meta.title: AgentPing Unification — Architecture Brief
meta.description: Merging GenUI + lev-now + dashboard-runner + websocket dispatch into one control plane

props.title: One Control Plane
props.subtitle: AgentPing absorbs GenUI, lev-now, dashboard-runner, and websocket dispatch into a unified surface

props.content: Three systems built independently are converging. The scanner discovers 75 projects. The dashboard-runner manages their processes. The lev-now engine renders their state as pages. The websocket layer dispatches lev exec for lifecycle transitions. This brief maps the merge.

props.label: CURRENT STATE
props.title: Three Separate Systems
props.subtitle: Built independently, now ready to converge

props.title: GenUI / lev-now
props.content:
**Rendering engine.** POST JSON spec, get HTML. Zero-dep markdown engine. File manifest auto-scaffold. 6 themes, 11 element types, 13 section variants.

**Key files:**
- `plugins/lev-now/src/engine/render.ts`
- `plugins/lev-now/src/handlers/manifest.ts`
- `plugins/lev-now/src/engine/markdown.ts`

**Status:** Produces the /now pages, session feedback, content dashboards. Not yet integrated with AgentPing.

props.title: Dashboard Runner
props.content:
**Process management.** Manages 9 dashboards with auto-restart, port finding, health monitoring. YAML-driven config.

**Key files:**
- `dashboard-runner/src/runner.ts`
- `dashboard-runner/src/spawn-runner.ts`
- `dashboard-runner/config/dashboards.yaml`

**Status:** Live on :3030. SpawnRunner ships both build-and-exit and detected→ready flows.

props.title: AgentPing Web UI
props.content:
**React dashboard.** dashboard-manager-ui with Apps section, Parity Registry, Setup button, lifecycle badges. Plus studio (Electron), web-ui (ping responder).

**Key files:**
- `dashboard-manager-ui/src/components/DashboardList.tsx`
- `dashboard-manager-ui/src/components/ParitySection.tsx`
- `dashboard-manager-ui/src/components/SetupPanel.tsx`

**Status:** Rendering 11 parity frameworks, 9 dashboards, filter chips, health dots.

props.label: TARGET STATE
props.title: Unified Control Plane
props.subtitle: AgentPing becomes the interface where everything converges

props.content:
graph TD
    Scanner["Workspace Scanner\n75 projects, 6 runtimes"]
    Config[".lev/config.yaml\nprojects: section"]
    Runner["Dashboard Runner\nSpawnRunner + health"]
    API["REST + WebSocket API\n:3030"]
    LevNow["lev-now Engine\nJSON → HTML"]
    UI["AgentPing Web UI\nReact dashboard"]
    Tmux["tmux panes\nA0's harness"]
    DevServer["lev-now dev server\nhot reload"]
    Setup["Setup Flow\nSSE + lev exec"]
    Review["Component Review\n400+ migrations"]
    Parity["Parity Registry\n11 frameworks"]

    Config --> Scanner
    Scanner --> Runner
    Scanner --> API
    Runner --> API
    API --> UI
    API --> Tmux
    API --> DevServer
    LevNow --> UI
    LevNow --> DevServer
    Setup --> Runner
    UI --> Setup
    UI --> Review
    UI --> Parity
props.title: Unified Architecture

props.content:
The scanner is the single source of truth. It reads `config.yaml`, detects all projects, and feeds the dashboard-runner (process management), the API (REST + WebSocket), and all three render targets (React UI, tmux panes, lev-now HTML).

**lev-now becomes a component library for AgentPing.** Instead of generating standalone pages, lev-now renders components INSIDE the dashboard. The parity cards, KPI rows, and project cards are already lev-now element types — they just need to render as React components instead of static HTML.

**Component review happens inline.** The 400+ migrated @kingly/ui components each get a card in the dashboard. GenUI renders variants, you approve/reject, lifecycle transitions. The dashboard IS the review tool.

props.label: CONVERGENCE PATH
props.title: What Moves Where
props.subtitle: What gets absorbed, what stays separate

props.label: 3-WAY VENN
props.title: Same Data, Three Surfaces
props.subtitle: Each surface is a projection of the same scanner + API output

props.title: Data Sources (shared)
props.content:
**Scanner output** — 75 projects, 6 runtimes, 6 frameworks
**Dashboard-runner API** — 9 dashboards, health, metrics, lifecycle
**Parity registry** — 11 frameworks, 88 features, adoption health
**.lev/config.yaml** — scan_dirs, self, external projects
**FlowMind sessions** — agent states (idle/running/paused/completed)
**Agent mail** — cross-agent coordination messages

props.title: Render Targets (separate)
props.content:
**AgentPing Web** (React) — interactive dashboard at :5175. Rich interactions, Setup button, parity filter chips, websocket streaming. Owned by A2.

**tmux panes** (terminal) — keyboard-driven workstream dashboard. NTM palette patterns. Floating panes via tmux display-popup. Owned by A0.

**lev-now HTML** (static) — deterministic page rendering with hot reload at :3456. Beautiful self-contained HTML. Content dashboards, session feedback, articles. Owned by A2 + A4.

props.label: MIGRATION SEQUENCE
props.title: Execution Order
props.subtitle: What to build next, in dependency order

items.title: Scanner + Dashboard Types
items.description: 75 projects detected, apps lane, lifecycle metadata, build-and-exit health check

items.title: Storybook Fix + Parity Section
items.description: SB8→SB10 migration (359 imports), 11 framework cards with filter chips and health dots

items.title: SpawnRunner + Setup Endpoint
items.description: Generalized spawn with StreamOp streaming. POST /setup dispatches lev exec, promotes lifecycle

items.title: Stats Endpoint + File Manifest
items.description: /api/projects/stats for A4. manifest.ts + markdown.ts for site generation

items.title: Card-Grid Projects View
items.description: Fork buildIndex() from lev-now. 75 projects as searchable cards with runtime/framework badges

items.title: lev-now React Wrapper
items.description: Render lev-now components as React elements inside dashboard-manager-ui. Convergence point.

items.title: Component Review Pipeline
items.description: 400+ @kingly/ui migrations as dashboard cards. GenUI renders variants. Approve/reject inline.

items.title: config.yaml as Single Source
items.description: dashboard-runner reads .lev/config.yaml projects: directly. Eliminates dashboards.yaml.

props.title: Delete packages/canvas/
props.content:
Arch verdict: delete (rm -rf). dcg blocked the command. Run manually:
`rm -rf community/agentping/packages/canvas/`
Package is DEPRECATED, builds disabled, zero consumers, merged into web-ui.

props.title: Component Review Entry Point
props.content: When we start the 400-component review: should each component be a bead (trackable lifecycle) or a dashboard card (visual triage)? Current state: 327 beads exist but are all OPEN awaiting human visual QA. Proposal: render each component via GenUI in the dashboard, approve/reject there, beads close automatically.
